title: Evening Campfire Gathering in El Bolsón Río Negro Amidst Rustic Patagonian Wood Construction
description: El Bolsón, Río Negro: A group of friends shares a warm, intimate evening around a crackling bonfire nestled beside the rugged exterior of a wooden structure. The golden, flickering light of the flames casts deep shadows across the clearing, highlighting the textures of the raw timber siding and the rough-hewn landscape. Audrey Bergner is visible resting near the fire, engaged in conversation while the ambient glow illuminates the swirling smoke and the faces of those present. Samuel Jeffery stands nearby, contributing to the convivial atmosphere as the group enjoys a slow-paced nocturnal ritual. The low-key lighting creates a high-contrast visual depth, emphasizing the raw, unpolished beauty of Patagonian living where the boundary between shelter and wilderness blurs. This scene captures the essence of communal life in the Andean foothills, where traditional outdoor gatherings serve as a focal point for connection and shared warmth. The presence of wood-fired cooking elements underscores the self-reliant and earthy lifestyle often found in this remote corner of Argentina. Such moments define the traveler’s experience in the region, bridging the gap between adventurous exploration and the quiet, grounding nature of a bonfire under the night sky. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
